extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won the Madrid sprint finale ahead of BENNETT Sam after DECEUNINCK-QuickStep strung out the peloton at 68-71 kph on the penultimate lap
tactical_patterns: CAVAGNA Rémi deliberately went into the red at 71kph before the hairpin on penultimate lap to string peloton single-file before swinging off
DECEUNINCK placed MAJKA at front for multiple laps to avoid washing machine positioning and control tempo without burning lead-out
DEGENKOLB John held the wheel through corner for KIRSCH Alex rather than leading sprint, inverting traditional UAE train hierarchy

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: Second Vuelta stage, won photo finish despite being boxed in at 500m, declared this most important career victory
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: Boxed in during final 500m with JAKOBSEN and lost second consecutive photo finish despite strong positioning
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Never genuinely challenged after Stage 10 ITT win despite crosswind pressure days, rock-solid team control throughout
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: At 20 years old, closed to 22 seconds behind VALVERDE for podium, marking Slovenia's historic double podium placement
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: positive: Tactical sacrifice stringing peloton at 71kph into hairpin on penultimate lap neutralized rival sprint trains
rider_signals.MAJKA Rafał: neutral: Deployed in atypical tempo-setting role for multiple laps rather than climbing domestique duties
rider_signals.KIRSCH Alex: neutral: UAE inverted sprint train with DEGENKOLB holding wheel through corner rather than leading KIRSCH out
